title: Spiking Vision Transformer with Saccadic Attention
authors: Not specified in the provided text

problem.description: Vision Transformers (ViTs) require significant computational and memory resources, restricting deployment in resource-constrained edge environments. Spiking Neural Networks (SNNs) are more energy-efficient but have performance gaps compared to ViTs.
specific_issues: Most SNN-based ViT models retain energy-intensive MAC operations
Approaches rely on Dot-Product operations not well-suited for binary spike characteristics
Self-attention is treated merely as an efficient token mixer
Focus on spatial feature extraction overlooking temporal dynamics

solution.description: Introduction of Saccadic Spike Self-Attention mechanism inspired by biological vision
key_features: Inspired by biological saccadic mechanisms that focus on specific visual areas dynamically
Offers higher energy and computational efficiency
Aligns with temporal characteristics of SNNs

key_innovation: Designing a Saccadic Spike Self-Attention paradigm tailored to the spatio-temporal characteristics of SNNs, moving beyond using self-attention merely as an efficient token mixer
